questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022, what is the primary purpose of establishing an Information Security Management System (ISMS)?
options: A. To establish a systematic approach for managing sensitive company information and mitigating information security risks
D. To comply exclusively with data protection regulations
B. To ensure all employees pass annual security awareness training
C. To implement firewalls and intrusion detection systems
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 4.1 defines ISMS purpose as establishing a systematic, risk-based approach to identify, manage, and mitigate information security risks aligned with business objectives.
questions.justification_wrong: Training, technical controls, and regulatory compliance are components or outcomes, not the primary foundational purpose of the ISMS structure defined in Clause 4.

questions.text: Which ISO 27002:2022 control category addresses the need for documented information security policies at the organizational level?
options: D. Technological controls (A.8)
C. Physical controls (A.7)
B. Organizational controls (A.5) - specifically control 5.1 on policies for information security
A. People controls (A.6)
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 control A.5.1 requires organizations to establish documented information security policies defining objectives, scope, and roles. This is fundamental organizational governance.
questions.justification_wrong: Controls A.6 focus on people competence and awareness; A.7 addresses physical security; A.8 covers technological measures. Policy establishment is organizational governance.

questions.text: In ISO 27001:2022, which clause requires organizations to determine the scope of their ISMS and document relevant inclusions/exclusions?
options: A. Clause 4.2 - Determining the scope of the ISMS
B. Clause 5.1 - Leadership and commitment
C. Clause 6.1 - Actions to address risks and opportunities
D. Clause 8.1 - Operational planning and control
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 4.2 explicitly requires organizations to determine ISMS scope considering business context, stakeholder needs, and applicable requirements, documenting decisions.
questions.justification_wrong: Clause 5.1 addresses leadership; Clause 6 covers risk treatment planning; Clause 8 addresses operational implementation. Scope determination is foundational in Clause 4.

questions.text: According to ISO 27002:2022, which people-related control requires organizations to ensure information security competence among personnel?
options: C. Control A.6.3 on information security awareness, education and training
A. Control A.6.1 on screening of personnel
B. Control A.6.2 on terms and conditions of employment
D. Control A.6.8 on information security incident reporting
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 control A.6.3 specifically mandates organizations provide awareness, education, and training to achieve appropriate competence levels for information security responsibilities.
questions.justification_wrong: A.6.1 addresses background verification; A.6.2 covers employment agreements; A.6.8 focuses on incident notification. Competence development is explicitly control A.6.3.

questions.text: In ISO 27001:2022 risk management context, what distinguishes risk treatment options of 'modify' and 'retain'?
options: D. Modify requires immediate implementation; retain requires annual reassessment
B. Modify means transferring risk to third parties; retain means eliminating the risk source
A. Modify involves implementing controls to reduce risk; retain involves accepting residual risk without further action
C. Modify applies only to technical risks; retain applies only to organizational risks
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 defines risk treatment: modification implements controls reducing likelihood/impact; retention accepts residual risk after evaluating whether it remains acceptable.
questions.justification_wrong: Risk transfer and avoidance are separate options. Risk treatment categories aren't segregated by risk type. Implementation timelines depend on organizational context, not treatment type classification.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022, which of the following best describes the primary purpose of establishing an information security management system (ISMS)?
options: C. To comply exclusively with legal and regulatory requirements
A. To preserve the confidentiality, integrity, and availability of information assets through systematic risk management
D. To implement the maximum number of ISO 27002 controls regardless of organizational context
B. To eliminate all information security risks completely
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 4.1 defines ISMS purpose as preserving CIA of information through systematic approach. Risk-based implementation ensures proportionate control deployment aligned with organizational context and risk appetite.
questions.justification_wrong: Complete risk elimination is impossible; compliance alone ignores business objectives. Indiscriminate control implementation contradicts ISO 27001's risk-based, context-dependent approach requiring threat and vulnerability analysis.

questions.text: In accordance with ISO 27002:2022, control A.5.1 (Policies for information security) requires that policies be reviewed and updated. What is the minimum recommended frequency?
options: A. Every month
B. At planned intervals or when significant changes occur
D. Annually without exception
C. Only when regulatory requirements change
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 A.5.1 specifies reviews at planned intervals and when significant organizational or regulatory changes occur, ensuring policy relevance without rigid fixed schedules that ignore contextual triggers.
questions.justification_wrong: Monthly reviews create unnecessary overhead. Regulatory-only triggers miss internal changes. Strict annual schedules ignore emerging threats and organizational evolution requiring timely policy adjustments.

questions.text: Which of the following best represents the relationship between ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27002:2022 in an ISMS implementation?
options: C. ISO 27001 defines requirements; ISO 27002 provides implementation guidance for applicable controls
B. ISO 27002 replaces ISO 27001 for organizations seeking certification
A. ISO 27001 is mandatory; ISO 27002 is optional guidance for control implementation
D. Both standards are identical and interchangeable for certification purposes
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 establishes ISMS requirements (normative); ISO 27002:2022 offers detailed, non-mandatory implementation guidance across 93 controls. Organizations select applicable controls aligned with risk assessment outcomes.
questions.justification_wrong: ISO 27002 is guidance, not optional escape; ISO 27001 remains certification requirement. ISO 27002 never replaces ISO 27001. Standards serve complementary roles: requirements-setting versus implementation-advising.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.2, when conducting a risk assessment, which aspect is NOT a primary component of the risk assessment methodology?
options: D. Annual audit of all IT vendors without exception
B. Evaluation of vulnerabilities and existing controls
A. Identification of information assets and threats
C. Determination of risk acceptance criteria and risk tolerance levels
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 6.2 requires risk assessment methodology address asset identification, threat-vulnerability analysis, and risk criteria. Mandatory vendor audits are operational procedures, not core methodology components.
questions.justification_wrong: Asset identification (A), vulnerability evaluation (B), and risk criteria determination (C) are explicitly required in clause 6.2. Vendor audits, while potentially necessary, aren't mandatory risk assessment methodology elements.

questions.text: In the context of ISO 27002:2022 People controls (A.6), which control specifically addresses the need for employees to understand information security responsibilities before commencing work?
options: A. A.6.2 Information security roles and responsibilities
D. A.6.5 Access control
C. A.6.1 Screening
B. A.6.3 Segregation of duties
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 A.6.2 explicitly requires clear assignment and communication of information security roles/responsibilities ensuring personnel understand expectations before employment commencement, supporting organizational accountability.
questions.justification_wrong: A.6.3 addresses conflict-of-interest prevention through duty separation. A.6.1 covers pre-employment verification. A.6.5 manages system access. None specifically mandate pre-commencement responsibility communication as A.6.2 does.

questions.text: According to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 and ISO/IEC 27002:2022, which control from the Organizational category is mandatory for establishing the foundational structure of an information security management system (ISMS)?
options: D. Security awareness training must be conducted quarterly for all stakeholders
B. Third-party risk assessments are required before any vendor engagement
A. Information security policies and procedures must be established, documented, and reviewed at least annually
C. Incident response teams must include external law enforcement representatives
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 5.2 requires documented information security policies as foundational. ISO 27002:2022 Control 5.1 mandates organizational policies establishing ISMS direction and commitment, essential for governance structure.
questions.justification_wrong: While vendor assessment (B), incident response composition (C), and training frequency (D) are important controls, they are not foundational requirements. Policy establishment precedes and enables all other controls.

questions.text: In the context of ISO/IEC 27002:2022 People controls, which approach best ensures sustained information security awareness and competence across an organization?
options: D. External auditors conducting quarterly training sessions exclusively
C. Security training mandated only for IT personnel and managers
A. One-time security training during employee onboarding is sufficient for compliance
B. Role-specific security awareness programs integrated into regular work processes with periodic reinforcement
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 Control 6.2 emphasizes role-specific awareness and competence with ongoing training. Integration into work processes ensures continuous reinforcement and behavioral change, supporting sustained security culture.
questions.justification_wrong: One-time training (A) is insufficient per ISO 27002. Limiting to IT/managers (C) ignores organizational coverage. External-only training (D) lacks internal responsibility and continuity required by Clause 6.3.

questions.text: Which physical security control from ISO/IEC 27002:2022 addresses the protection of facilities housing critical information assets?
options: D. Regular penetration testing by external security consultants
B. Perimeter security with controlled entry points, surveillance, and visitor management
C. Implementation of multi-factor authentication for all system users
A. Encryption of all data at rest and in transit to prevent unauthorized access
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 Controls 7.1-7.7 address physical security including perimeter controls (7.1), entry points (7.2), visitor management (7.3), and monitoring (7.4), forming comprehensive facility protection.
questions.justification_wrong: Encryption (A) is technological control, not physical. MFA (C) is logical access control. Penetration testing (D) is assessment activity, not physical security control per ISO 27002:2022 classification.

questions.text: According to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 8.2.3 and ISO/IEC 27002:2022, how should an organization determine the scope of its ISMS during the initial implementation phase?
options: C. Scope is automatically defined by industry regulations and sector-specific standards
B. Scope determination requires considering organizational structure, products, services, location, and dependencies with stakeholder involvement
D. Scope includes only assets with direct revenue generation potential
A. Scope should include only the IT department and critical digital systems
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.2.3 requires scope determination considering organization, structure, products, services, locations, and dependencies. ISO 27002 Control 5.14 emphasizes stakeholder consultation for appropriate scope definition.
questions.justification_wrong: Limiting to IT only (A) excludes organizational scope requirements. Relying solely on regulations (C) ignores organizational context. Revenue focus (D) contradicts risk-based approach mandated by ISO 27001:2022.

questions.text: Which technological control from ISO/IEC 27002:2022 is essential for protecting information systems from unauthorized logical access?
options: D. Implementation of annual third-party security audits and certifications
C. Establishment of incident response procedures and forensic investigation teams
B. User identification, authentication mechanisms, and access control policies with regular review
A. Physical locks and security cameras installed at data center entrances
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 Control 8.1-8.3 mandate user identification (8.1), authentication (8.2), and access right management (8.3) with periodic review, forming logical access protection framework.
questions.justification_wrong: Physical locks (A) are physical controls. Incident response (C) addresses post-breach management, not access prevention. Audits (D) are assessment activities, not preventive logical access controls.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27002:2022, when establishing an Information Security Management System (ISMS), which organizational control directly addresses the alignment between business objectives and security risk management?
options: A. Control 5.1 (Policies for information security) - establishes documented direction aligned with business context and risk appetite
B. Control 6.1 (Information security roles and responsibilities) - assigns accountability for security implementation
D. Control 8.1 (Screening) - verifies competence of security personnel
C. Control 7.1 (General awareness and understanding) - trains personnel on security policies
questions.justification_correct: Control 5.1 requires organizations to establish comprehensive information security policies reflecting business objectives, legal requirements, and risk context. This foundational control ensures ISMS strategy aligns with organizational goals per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 5.2.
questions.justification_wrong: Controls 6.1, 7.1, and 8.1 support implementation and awareness but do not directly establish the strategic alignment between business objectives and security risk management framework.

questions.text: In ISO 27002:2022, which control from the People domain ensures that information security responsibilities are clearly communicated and understood across the organization before incidents occur?
options: C. Control 8.2 (Information security induction, training and awareness) - develops competence after hiring
D. Control 8.3 (Information security awareness campaigns) - periodic awareness initiatives
B. Control 7.3 (Information security in recruitment) - screens candidates during hiring
A. Control 6.2 (Information security responsibilities) - defines and communicates roles and accountability before incidents
questions.justification_correct: Control 6.2 requires clear definition and communication of information security responsibilities before operations begin. This preventive control ensures accountability and understanding, reducing human error risks per ISO 27002:2022 A.6.2.
questions.justification_wrong: Controls 7.3, 8.2, and 8.3 address training, awareness, or recruitment screening but occur after or during role assignment, not establishing foundational responsibility clarity upfront.

questions.text: An organization implements segregation of duties (SoD) across its financial systems. Under ISO 27002:2022 Technical controls, which control specifically addresses this preventive measure against unauthorized transactions?
options: A. Control 8.3 (Segregation of duties) - prevents single individuals from performing incompatible security-relevant functions
B. Control 8.4 (Access control) - manages user access based on least privilege principles
C. Control 8.5 (Authentication) - verifies user identity through credentials
D. Control 8.6 (Capacity management) - monitors system resources and performance
questions.justification_correct: Control A.8.3 (Segregation of Duties) mandates that incompatible functions—such as authorization and execution of sensitive transactions—be assigned to different individuals, preventing fraud and unauthorized actions per ISO 27002:2022.
questions.justification_wrong: Controls 8.4, 8.5, and 8.6 address access management, authentication, and resource management respectively, but do not specifically prevent unauthorized actions through functional separation.

questions.text: During an ISO 27001:2022 gap analysis, an organization discovers that its data center lacks environmental controls. Which Physical domain control must be prioritized to protect information assets from environmental threats such as temperature, humidity, and water damage?
options: C. Control 11.3 (Securing and disposing of assets) - protects equipment from environmental hazards
D. Control 11.4 (Utilities) - provides protection against environmental threats and disruptions
A. Control 11.1 (Perimeter security) - restricts unauthorized physical access to facilities
B. Control 11.2 (Physical entry) - monitors and logs entry points to secure areas
questions.justification_correct: Control A.11.4 (Utilities) requires organizations to identify, design, provide, and manage utilities (power, water, heating) to protect information systems from environmental hazards per ISO 27002:2022 A.11.4.
questions.justification_wrong: Controls 11.1 and 11.2 focus on perimeter and entry control; Control 11.3 addresses asset disposal and security but not environmental protection infrastructure like HVAC and water management systems.

questions.text: An organization undergoes ISO 27001:2022 certification audit. The auditor requests evidence that the organization periodically reviews its information security risk assessment. Which Organizational control demonstrates compliance with this requirement?
options: B. Control 6.2 (Information security risk assessment) - identifies and evaluates information security risks at planned intervals
C. Control 6.3 (Information security risk treatment) - addresses identified risks through mitigation strategies
A. Control 5.3 (Integration of information security in organizational processes) - embeds security into business processes
D. Control 5.1 (Policies for information security) - documents organizational security direction
questions.justification_correct: Control A.6.2 requires organizations to conduct information security risk assessments at planned intervals or when significant changes occur, evaluating likelihood and impact of identified risks per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.1.3.
questions.justification_wrong: Control 6.3 addresses risk treatment, Control 5.3 integrates security into processes, and Control 5.1 documents policies—none specifically mandate periodic risk assessment review intervals.

questions.text: According to ISO 27001:2022 and ISO 27002:2022, when implementing control 5.23 (Information security for use of cloud services), which approach best addresses the residual risk after contractual obligations are established with a cloud service provider?
options: C. Ignore residual risk since the provider assumes all responsibility under SLA agreements
D. Conduct annual audits only without implementing any interim compensating controls
B. Eliminate all residual risk by transferring it entirely to the cloud provider through liability clauses
A. Accept residual risk through documented risk acceptance and implement compensating controls for identified gaps
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 requires organizations to assess and treat risks, including residual risks. Compensating controls and documented risk acceptance are normative approaches when full risk elimination is unfeasible, maintaining accountability within the organization's information security management system.
questions.justification_wrong: Risk cannot be entirely transferred contractually; organizations retain responsibility. SLAs do not eliminate residual risk. Annual audits alone are insufficient for continuous risk management per ISO 27001 requirements.

questions.text: In the context of ISO 27002:2022 control 6.7 (Threat intelligence), an organization discovers that its threat intelligence source has become compromised. What is the most appropriate immediate action aligned with ISMS principles?
options: B. Immediately cease using the source, conduct impact assessment, and re-establish intelligence feeds from verified sources
A. Continue using the source while implementing additional validation mechanisms and updating the threat intelligence control procedures
C. Reduce the criticality classification of intelligence from this source without changing operational procedures
D. Notify only senior management without updating detection or prevention controls based on this intelligence
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 and ISO 27001:2022 require immediate discontinuation of compromised information sources. Impact assessment and re-establishment from verified sources align with Annex A control requirements for maintaining intelligence integrity and organizational resilience.
questions.justification_wrong: Continuing use with validation introduces systemic risk. Reducing criticality masks the threat. Limiting notifications violates the principle of proportionate response to identified security weaknesses per ISMS governance.

questions.text: Which combination of controls from ISO 27002:2022 categories (Organizational, People, Physical, Technological) is most effective for implementing a Zero Trust architecture aligned with ISO 27001:2022 principles?
options: C. Control 8.22 (Monitoring) exclusively, with quarterly manual reviews
D. Control 5.16 (Identity management) and 8.1 (User endpoint devices) without compensating controls
A. Control 5.15 (Access control) + 6.8 (Secure development) + 5.16 (Identity management) only
B. Control 5.15 + 6.8 + 5.16 + 6.7 (Threat intelligence) + 5.23 (Cloud services) + 8.22 (Monitoring) + 6.1 (Screening)
questions.justification_correct: Zero Trust requires layered controls spanning all domains: identity/access (5.15, 5.16), secure development (6.8), threat intelligence (6.7), cloud security (5.23), continuous monitoring (8.22), and personnel screening (6.1). This comprehensive approach aligns with ISO 27001:2022 risk-based treatment strategy.
questions.justification_wrong: Single-domain approaches lack the comprehensive coverage required. Exclusive monitoring without preventive controls violates defense-in-depth principles. Omitting compensating controls creates exploitable gaps in the architecture.

questions.text: When conducting a control 5.1 (Policies for information security) review under ISO 27001:2022, an organization identifies that a policy has become obsolete due to organizational restructuring. What is the correct treatment per ISMS requirements?
options: C. Keep the policy active but mark it as 'deprecated' without formal withdrawal or communication
A. Archive the policy and draft a new one, then follow the approval workflow, communicate changes, and update the policy register
D. Replace it informally with verbal instructions from management to ensure flexibility
B. Immediately delete the policy from all systems and notify stakeholders via email
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 requires formal policy management including documented review, approval by appropriate authority, communication, and maintenance of policy records. Archiving the old policy, creating a successor, following approval processes, and updating registers ensures governance and traceability.
questions.justification_wrong: Deletion without documentation violates record-keeping requirements. Deprecated status without formal withdrawal creates confusion. Verbal instructions lack enforceability and auditability required by ISMS governance frameworks.

questions.text: Under ISO 27002:2022, when implementing control 6.5 (Managing user access rights), which scenario requires immediate escalation and risk treatment modification?
options: A. A contractor's access privileges exceed their documented role requirements for 15 days pending renewal documentation
D. A department head delegates access approval authority to their administrative assistant temporarily
B. A privileged user requests access to systems unrelated to their business function, citing 'operational efficiency'
C. Annual access reviews confirm all active users' rights remain appropriate per segregation of duties policies
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 requires access rights to align with 'need-to-know' principles and segregation of duties. Inappropriate privileged access requests violate least privilege. Immediate escalation, business justification validation, and compensating controls are mandated responses to non-compliant access requests.
questions.justification_wrong: Short-term overprovisioning with documented renewal is manageable. Confirmatory reviews demonstrate control effectiveness. Temporary delegation requires authorization but does not automatically trigger escalation if properly governed and monitored.

questions.text: An organization implements ISO 27001:2022 and must establish risk treatment plans. According to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 Clause 6.2, which of the following BEST represents the primary objective of risk treatment options?
options: D. To transfer all risks to insurance providers to avoid organizational responsibility
B. To eliminate all identified information security risks through immediate implementation of all controls
A. To modify, retain, avoid, or share risks to achieve acceptable residual risk levels aligned with organizational risk appetite
C. To ensure external auditors have documented evidence of control implementation
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 mandates risk treatment decisions: modify (implement controls), retain (accept), avoid (eliminate activity), or share (outsource). Treatment must align with risk criteria and organizational context. Complete elimination is impractical; insurance is only one sharing mechanism.
questions.justification_wrong: Complete risk elimination is unrealistic; external audit is secondary objective; insurance alone doesn't constitute proper risk treatment per normative framework requirements.

questions.text: During a lead implementer assessment, an organization's ISMS scope excludes a subsidiary with significant data processing activities. What is the PRIMARY concern regarding this scoping decision per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 4.3?
options: D. The organization may exclude subsidiaries if they operate in lower-risk industries than the parent organization
A. The exclusion violates Clause 4.3 if information assets of the subsidiary are interconnected with the organization's main ISMS and pose risks
B. Subsidiaries must always be included regardless of information security risks or operational independence
C. Scoping decisions are entirely discretionary and require no justification in the Statement of Applicability
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 4.3 requires clear scope definition with documented rationale. Exclusions are permissible only if justified; interconnected data flows and shared infrastructure may mandate inclusion. Risk-based assessment is required, not industry classification alone.
questions.justification_wrong: Mandatory inclusion ignores legitimate exclusion criteria; discretionary scoping without justification violates Clause 4.3 requirements; industry classification doesn't determine information security risk adequately.

questions.text: An organization implements ISO 27001 and must select controls from ISO/IEC 27002:2022. When evaluating Organizational controls (37 controls), which approach BEST aligns with Clause 5.1 (Leadership and commitment)?
options: A. Select controls A.5.1 through A.5.7 focusing on organizational policies, roles, responsibilities, and competence frameworks that demonstrate executive commitment
D. Prioritize only IT-related organizational controls while deferring governance and compliance controls to external consultants
B. Implement all 37 organizational controls uniformly across all organizational units regardless of assessed risks
C. Rely exclusively on ISO 27001:2022 Annex A without analyzing risk assessment outcomes for control justification
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 5.1 emphasizes leadership commitment through governance structures. ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Organizational controls (A.5.1-A.5.7) address policies, roles, competence, and accountability—foundational for demonstrating executive engagement required by normative requirements.
questions.justification_wrong: Uniform implementation ignores risk assessment outputs; SoA requires risk-based justification; all control domains require organizational ownership; external deferral contradicts internal commitment principle.

questions.text: During internal audit preparation, an organization discovers non-conformity in access control implementation (ISO/IEC 27002:2022 A.8.2 - User access management). What is the CORRECT first action per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.3 (Control of externally provided processes)?
options: C. Report only high-severity findings to management while resolving minor non-conformities informally without documentation
D. Assign audit findings exclusively to IT security personnel without involving process owners or management representatives
A. Document the non-conformity root cause, implement corrective action with timelines, establish preventive controls, and verify effectiveness through follow-up reviews
B. Immediately terminate all external service provider contracts without investigation to eliminate external risk exposure
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.3 requires documented non-conformity management with root cause analysis, corrective actions, timelines, and effectiveness verification. Systematic approach ensures control improvements and prevents recurrence through preventive measures.
questions.justification_wrong: Immediate contract termination bypasses root cause analysis; informal resolution violates audit requirements; selective reporting undermines management oversight; IT-only assignment ignores accountability framework requirements.

questions.text: An organization achieves ISO 27001:2022 certification and must maintain ISMS effectiveness per Clause 9.1 (Monitoring, measurement, analysis and evaluation). Which combination BEST ensures continuous improvement?
options: A. Establish KPIs for control effectiveness, conduct quarterly management reviews analyzing trends, update risk assessment annually, and implement improvements based on audit and incident data
C. Monitor only critical controls while eliminating oversight of supporting controls to reduce operational burden
D. Conduct internal audits reactively only after security incidents occur rather than establishing proactive monitoring schedules
B. Perform annual certification audits exclusively and defer all monitoring activities to external certification bodies until re-certification
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.1 mandates continuous monitoring with KPIs, trend analysis through management reviews, regular risk reassessment, and incident-driven improvements. Holistic approach ensures sustained effectiveness and organizational alignment with evolving threats.
questions.justification_wrong: External-only monitoring violates internal responsibility principle; selective control oversight creates blind spots; reactive auditing delays issue detection; certification audits are verification points, not continuous monitoring substitutes.

questions.text: During an ISO 27001:2022 implementation, you discover that access control lists (ACLs) are not regularly reviewed. According to ISO/IEC 27002:2022, which control directly addresses this requirement?
options: C. Control 7.1 (Physical Entry) addresses only physical access controls
B. Control 6.5 (Supplier Relationships) mandates quarterly access reviews
A. Control 5.15 (Access Control) requires periodic review and adjustment of user access rights
D. Control 8.2 (User Endpoint Devices) focuses solely on device management
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Control 5.15 explicitly requires organizations to grant, review, and revoke access rights based on business and information security requirements. Regular reviews ensure least privilege principle adherence.
questions.justification_wrong: Control 6.5 concerns supplier management; Control 7.1 addresses physical security only; Control 8.2 focuses on endpoint device security, not access control reviews.

questions.text: Your organization implements a classification scheme for information assets. Under ISO/IEC 27002:2022, what is the primary objective of this control?
options: A. To establish secure labeling requirements and handling procedures based on sensitivity levels
C. To reduce compliance audit costs by simplifying documentation
B. To eliminate the need for encryption across all systems
D. To create a single security baseline applicable to all information types
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Control 5.12 (Information Classification) mandates classifying information to determine appropriate protection measures. Classification drives handling, storage, and transmission requirements aligned with asset sensitivity.
questions.justification_wrong: Classification does not eliminate encryption needs, reduce audit costs only, or create uniform baselines. It enables differentiated controls proportional to asset value and sensitivity.

questions.text: An employee leaves the organization. According to ISO/IEC 27002:2022, which control ensures proper deprovisioning of access and return of assets?
options: D. Control 8.4 (Removal of Access Rights) applies only to contractors
B. Control 5.1 (Policies for Information Security) addresses only policy creation
A. Control 6.3 (Termination or Change of Employment) requires documented termination procedures including access removal and asset return
C. Control 7.2 (Physical Security Perimeter) manages building access only
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Control 6.3 mandates documented termination procedures encompassing access rights removal, credential retrieval, asset return, and confidentiality obligation continuation for all employment changes.
questions.justification_wrong: Control 5.1 covers policy framework; Control 7.2 addresses physical perimeter; Control 8.4 applies broadly, not solely contractors. Control 6.3 is the dedicated termination control.

questions.text: You are designing a data backup strategy compliant with ISO/IEC 27002:2022. Which control requires testing restore procedures?
options: B. Control 8.14 (Data Backup) focuses only on backup frequency, not restoration validation
D. Control 7.4 (Physical Security Monitoring) addresses surveillance of backup locations only
A. Control 5.30 (ICT Readiness for Business Continuity) includes testing and maintaining backup restoration capabilities
C. Control 5.23 (Information Security for Supplier Relationships) manages third-party backups only
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Control 5.30 requires organizations to test ICT readiness for business continuity regularly, including backup restoration capabilities and recovery time objectives validation.
questions.justification_wrong: Control 8.14 addresses backup procedures; Control 5.23 concerns supplier relationships; Control 7.4 focuses on monitoring. Control 5.30 explicitly mandates restoration testing.

questions.text: Your organization conducts an internal audit and identifies that incident response procedures lack documented roles and responsibilities. Which ISO/IEC 27002:2022 control addresses this gap?
options: B. Control 5.1 (Policies for Information Security) mandates only high-level policy statements
A. Control 5.25 (Incident Management) requires establishing a coordinated incident response plan with defined roles and responsibilities
C. Control 6.6 (Contractor Management) addresses third-party incident procedures exclusively
D. Control 8.32 (Change Management) focuses solely on technical change control procedures
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 Control 5.25 mandates organizations establish documented incident management procedures including assigned roles, responsibilities, escalation paths, and communication protocols for effective incident response.
questions.justification_wrong: Control 5.1 provides policy framework only; Control 6.6 concerns contractor management; Control 8.32 addresses change management. Control 5.25 is the dedicated incident management control.

questions.text: During an ISO 27001:2022 implementation, you must establish risk ownership accountability. According to ISO 27001 Annex A and ISO 27005, which organizational role should formally own identified information security risks and approve risk treatment decisions?
options: D. Junior IT support staff member assigned to asset inventory maintenance
A. Chief Information Security Officer (CISO) or equivalent senior management representative
C. External third-party risk assessment consultant hired on contract
B. Information Security Technical Administrator responsible for system patches
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 clause 5.3 requires top management to assign CISO or equivalent role for risk ownership and accountability. ISO 27005 reinforces this through risk owner designation ensuring authority and responsibility alignment.
questions.justification_wrong: Technical administrators, external consultants, and junior staff lack organizational authority to approve treatment plans. Risk ownership requires senior management mandate per governance requirements.

questions.text: Your organization processes sensitive personal data across multiple jurisdictions. When implementing control A.5.23 (Data Retention) from ISO 27002:2022, what is the PRIMARY consideration that distinguishes data retention decisions from data deletion decisions?
options: A. Legal, regulatory, and business requirements determining retention periods before secure deletion
D. The encryption algorithm strength applied to archived datasets
B. The physical storage media type (SSD vs. HDD) used for archival purposes
C. The number of backup copies maintained in geographic locations
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 A.5.23 and GDPR Article 5(1)(e) mandate retention based on legal obligations and business needs. Retention periods precede deletion; neither storage media type, backup quantity, nor encryption strength determine retention necessity.
questions.justification_wrong: Storage media, backup copies, and encryption are implementation mechanisms, not determinants of retention requirements. Legal/regulatory compliance drives retention policy.

questions.text: During control A.8.34 (Secure Development Life Cycle - SDLC) implementation, you identify that developers lack awareness of secure coding practices. Which preventive control from ISO 27002:2022 Organizational theme should be prioritized to address this gap BEFORE development activities commence?
options: D. A.7.14 Physical Entry - restricting developer office access
C. A.8.32 Change Log - recording all code modification history
B. A.6.6 Information Security Awareness, Education and Training - mandatory secure coding curriculum
A. A.6.7 Threat Intelligence - monitoring external threat databases
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 A.6.6 mandates awareness training before role assignment. Developers require secure coding knowledge prerequisite. Training prevents vulnerabilities at source before implementing A.8.34 SDLC controls.
questions.justification_wrong: Threat intelligence, change logs, and physical access controls are reactive or environmental measures. They do not address skill gaps preventing secure development practices.

questions.text: Your organization implements control A.7.13 (Clear Desk and Clear Screen) across office environments. A senior executive argues this control is excessive for confidential business strategy documents kept in locked drawers. What is the CORRECT justification per ISO 27002:2022 to mandate compliance without exception?
options: A. The control applies universally regardless of role or document classification to prevent unauthorized observation
B. Executive roles are exempt from clear desk requirements by ISO 27001 framework
D. Locked drawers eliminate need for clear desk practices in executive areas
C. Only classified government documents require clear screen protection, not business documents
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 A.7.13 states clear desk/screen applies to all personnel without role exemption. Prevents unauthorized observation regardless of individual sensitivity assessment. Locked storage complements, not replaces, clear desk practice.
questions.justification_wrong: ISO 27001 contains no executive exemptions. Business confidentiality equals government classification for control purposes. Locked drawers do not prevent incidental observation of visible materials.

questions.text: During internal audit of control A.8.1 (User Endpoint Devices) implementation, you discover mobile devices accessing critical systems lack Mobile Device Management (MDM) enforcement. What PRIMARY risk assessment conclusion aligns with ISO 27005 risk methodology?
options: C. Risk is eliminated by instructing users to avoid public Wi-Fi and use strong passwords only
D. Risk is negligible since devices connect through corporate firewalls protecting backend systems
B. Risk is unmitigated; unauthorized apps, malware, and data loss exposure exists without MDM controls
A. Risk is acceptable because employees bring personal devices voluntarily and intend no harm
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27005 risk = likelihood × impact. MDM absence creates high likelihood of malware infection, credential theft, and unauthorized data access. Intent irrelevant; technical controls mitigate technical vulnerabilities regardless of user behavior.
questions.justification_wrong: Good intent does not prevent compromise. Passwords and firewall are insufficient endpoint protections. MDM provides application whitelisting, encryption, and remote wipe—fundamentals for mobile access risk mitigation.

questions.text: According to ISO/IEC 27001:2022 and ISO/IEC 27002:2022, which control attribute is fundamental for establishing accountability in information security governance within an organization?
options: D. Periodic rotation of security personnel to prevent conflicts of interest
B. Implementation of only technical security measures without documentation
C. Delegation of all security decisions to external consultants
A. Roles and responsibilities clearly defined and communicated across all organizational levels
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 A.5.1 requires clear definition and communication of roles and responsibilities. This establishes accountability framework essential for effective governance and control implementation across organizational structures.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B, C, D represent incomplete or inappropriate governance approaches. Technical measures alone lack accountability framework. External delegation removes organizational responsibility. Personnel rotation alone does not establish accountability structures.

questions.text: In the context of ISO/IEC 27002:2022 People controls, what is the primary objective of security awareness and competence training programs?
options: B. To create a compliance checklist for annual audits without behavioral change expectations
A. To ensure employees understand security policies and can recognize threats, reducing human-related security incidents
C. To replace technical security controls with procedural knowledge only
D. To document employee attendance records for HR performance reviews
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 A.6.2 emphasizes awareness and competence programs developing understanding and skills to support information security objectives. Effective training reduces human error and increases threat recognition capability.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B, C, D misrepresent training objectives. Compliance checklists ignore behavioral outcomes. Training cannot replace technical controls. Attendance documentation is administrative, not objective. Training must drive behavioral change.

questions.text: Which physical security control principle from ISO/IEC 27002:2022 is most critical for protecting against unauthorized physical access to cryptographic key storage areas?
options: D. Implementing camera surveillance without access control mechanisms
C. Restricting physical access to executive management only
A. Implementation of multi-layered access controls including authentication, monitoring, and environmental protection
B. Installing standard office locks on all doors regardless of asset sensitivity
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 A.11.1 requires layered physical security with authentication mechanisms, monitoring systems, and environmental controls for sensitive areas. This defense-in-depth approach protects cryptographic material against unauthorized access.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B, C, D represent inadequate controls. Standard locks lack authentication. Executive-only access ignores operational security needs. Surveillance alone lacks preventive access control. Multi-layered approach mandatory for cryptographic areas.

questions.text: According to ISO/IEC 27002:2022, what is the relationship between access control principles and the principle of least privilege in technological controls?
options: C. Access control and least privilege are independent concepts requiring separate implementation frameworks
B. Least privilege applies only to system administrators and external contractors
A. Least privilege ensures users receive minimum necessary permissions aligned to job functions, supporting risk-based access control implementation
D. Least privilege requirements override organizational policy requirements for operational efficiency
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27002:2022 A.8.2 establishes least privilege as foundational access control principle. Users granted minimum necessary permissions aligned to roles reduces attack surface and unauthorized access risks systematically.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B, C, D misunderstand least privilege scope and relationship. Principle applies organization-wide, not selectively. Access control and least privilege are integrated, not independent. Organizational policies must support, not contradict least privilege.

questions.text: In ISO/IEC 27001:2022 context, how should organizations approach the integration of technological security controls with organizational governance frameworks?
options: A. Establish technology control objectives aligned to information security policy, risk assessment outcomes, and organizational strategy with documented ownership
B. Implement controls based on vendor recommendations without organizational risk assessment alignment
D. Prioritize control cost reduction over alignment with organizational risk appetite and objectives
C. Separate technology controls from governance oversight for operational efficiency and independence
questions.justification_correct: ISO/IEC 27001:2022 requires integrated approach where technological controls support organizational objectives through policy alignment, risk assessment integration, and clear responsibility assignment. This ensures controls address actual organizational risks effectively.
questions.justification_wrong: Options B, C, D contradict integration principles. Vendor-driven implementation ignores organizational context. Separating technology from governance creates misalignment. Cost prioritization over risk management violates risk-based approach foundation.

questions.text: During an ISO 27001:2022 implementation, you must integrate controls from multiple domains. When designing the ISMS, which approach best ensures alignment between Organizational controls (A.5), People controls (A.6), Physical controls (A.7), and Technological controls (A.8)?
options: B. Deploy controls sequentially by domain, starting with technological controls as they provide the strongest security foundation.
D. Implement controls independently per domain to avoid complexity and ensure each domain operates autonomously.
A. Implement all 93 controls simultaneously across domains using a single unified risk assessment framework that maps interdependencies between control objectives.
C. Focus exclusively on organizational controls first, then apply physical and technological controls without re-evaluating people controls.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 4.3 (Risk Assessment) and A.1 (Governance) mandate integrated risk-based approach. Mapping control interdependencies across organizational (37), people (8), physical (14), and technological (34) controls ensures holistic ISMS alignment per ISO 27002:2022 guidance.
questions.justification_wrong: Sequential or independent domain implementation violates integrated ISMS principle. Technological controls alone cannot mitigate people-related risks. ISO 27001:2022 requires systematic, risk-driven control selection across all domains simultaneously.

questions.text: You are conducting a gap analysis for a financial institution's ISMS. Control A.5.1 (Policies for information security) is partially implemented, while A.6.1 (Screening) lacks formal documentation. How should you prioritize remediation considering interdependencies?
options: D. Defer both controls pending executive approval, as policy and screening are optional for financial institutions.
C. Address both simultaneously without sequencing, as they are independent controls in different domains.
B. Remediate A.5.1 first; established policies create framework for A.6.1 implementation and ensure consistent screening criteria per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 5.2.
A. Prioritize A.6.1 immediately because people controls prevent insider threats, which are statistically more damaging.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 5.2 (Information Security Policies) establishes governance framework. ISO 27002:2022 A.5.1 policy controls enable consistent implementation of people controls (A.6). Proper policies define screening criteria, competency requirements, and role-based responsibilities essential for A.6.1.
questions.justification_wrong: Statistical risk alone does not determine control sequencing. Neither control is optional for financial institutions. Controls exist in intentional hierarchy; foundational organizational controls enable downstream people/technical control effectiveness.

questions.text: An organization implements Physical control A.7.1 (Perimeter security) and must ensure consistency with Technological control A.8.1 (User endpoint devices). Which governance mechanism best ensures these controls remain synchronized throughout the ISMS lifecycle?
options: B. Create an integrated control ownership matrix with cross-domain dependencies documented in the ISMS procedures, reviewed quarterly per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.6.
A. Establish separate control review schedules for physical and technological domains to maintain independence and prevent conflicts.
D. Implement controls once during initial ISMS establishment; ongoing synchronization is unnecessary after certification.
C. Assign physical controls to facilities management and technological controls to IT, allowing each department autonomous decision-making.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.6 (Evaluation of Compliance) requires periodic review of control effectiveness. Cross-domain control ownership matrices ensure physical perimeter security integrates with endpoint device policies, preventing gaps. Quarterly reviews maintain synchronization per ISO 27002:2022 principles.
questions.justification_wrong: Separate schedules create asynchronous control drift. Autonomous department decisions risk conflicting requirements. Controls require continuous monitoring; one-time implementation violates ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.6 ongoing evaluation mandate and PDCA cycle principles.

questions.text: During internal audit planning for ISO 27001:2022 compliance, you must allocate audit resources across 93 controls. Using risk-based sampling and considering control dependencies, which strategy optimizes audit effectiveness?
options: B. Weight audits by risk criticality and control interdependencies: prioritize foundational organizational controls (A.5), then people (A.6), then physical/technological; include dependency-critical controls in all audit cycles per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2.
C. Focus exclusively on technological controls (A.8, 34 controls) as they pose highest residual risk regardless of organizational context.
A. Audit all 93 controls annually with equal emphasis, as ISO 27001:2022 requires 100% control coverage per certification requirements.
D. Audit controls randomly to ensure auditor objectivity and prevent predetermined outcomes.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2 (Internal Audit) requires risk-based internal audit programs. Foundational controls (A.5 organizational governance) enable downstream controls. Weighting by criticality and dependencies optimizes resource allocation while ensuring control effectiveness per ISO 27002:2022 hierarchy.
questions.justification_wrong: Equal emphasis wastes resources on low-risk controls. 100% annual coverage is neither required nor efficient. Technological-only focus ignores upstream organizational/people control failures. Random selection contradicts risk-based audit principles mandated in ISO 27001:2022 Clause 9.2.

questions.text: An organization's asset inventory (A.5.9) contains 5,000 IT assets. Controls A.8.2 (Media handling) and A.8.3 (Cryptography) depend on accurate asset classification. What is the most effective control design to maintain data integrity across these interconnected controls?
options: A. Maintain separate asset databases for media handling and cryptography; updates are manual and independent per control requirement.
D. Classify assets once during initial implementation; reclassification requires formal change control only if controls fail.

B. Implement single integrated asset management system with role-based access, automated classification rules, and audit trails; synchronized updates ensure A.5.9, A.8.2, A.8.3 consistency per ISO 27002:2022 A.8.1 principles.
C. Delegate asset inventory ownership to external third-party provider; internal controls A.8.2 and A.8.3 operate independently without inventory dependency.
questions.justification_correct: ISO 27002:2022 A.8.1 (User endpoint devices) and A.5.9 (Inventory) emphasize integrated asset management. Single source-of-truth systems prevent classification inconsistencies between media handling and cryptography controls. Automated rules and audit trails ensure compliance with ISO 27001:2022 Clause 8.5 control implementation requirements.
questions.justification_wrong: Separate databases create synchronization failures and data inconsistency. Third-party delegation removes control accountability. One-time classification violates continuous monitoring principles. Asset classification must evolve with organizational risk changes per ISO 27001:2022 Clause 6.2 (Risk Assessment).
